user_query:
I’m the older brother in our house, and I keep a running debate with my younger siblings about who has the best Western TV characters. Please set up a small, local, event-triggered pipeline that watches input/inbox for any *.csv watch logs (process files present at startup and any new ones that appear while it runs), validates each CSV using the provided tools/validate_watchlog.py (capture both stdout and stderr), and then aggregates only the valid rows to update two outputs:

1) output/character_tally.json — a JSON object with:
   - totals: a mapping of character name -> count across all valid rows
   - files_processed: a list of CSV file names processed in this run
   - invalid_rows: a mapping of file name -> list of invalid line numbers (as reported by the validator)
   - favorites: a mapping of favorite character name -> count across valid rows (favorites are defined in input/preferences/favorites.json)

2) output/weekly_debate_update.md — a short status report that includes:
   - Processed files and total row counts (valid vs invalid) per file
   - A Diagnostics section summarizing validator results using the captured command output/error messages (group by file and list line numbers and brief messages)
   - Top 3 characters by mentions across valid rows
   - A Favorites head-to-head section that compares the counts for the three favorites in input/preferences/favorites.json and states who is currently leading for this batch

Constraints and requirements:
- Use tools/validate_watchlog.py as the source of truth for validity. Exclude any rows whose line numbers appear in its stderr lines of the form: "ERROR in <path> on line N: ...". Do not re-implement your own validation logic; base row exclusion on the validator’s reported line numbers.
- Capture and analyze the validator’s stdout/stderr for each file. Persist the full combined validator output to output/logs/validator_output.txt and use it to populate the Diagnostics section in the MD report.
- Extract the structured data from the CSVs to compute the character counts; then read input/preferences/favorites.json to compute the favorites head-to-head summary.
- The watcher should process files present at startup and keep running to process any new matching CSV files added to input/inbox. Re-running your pipeline should overwrite output artifacts deterministically based on the CSVs currently present.
- Inputs: all CSVs under input/inbox/*.csv, input/preferences/favorites.json, and tools/validate_watchlog.py.
- Outputs: output/character_tally.json, output/weekly_debate_update.md, and output/logs/validator_output.txt.

If all goes well, I’ll be able to drop new watch logs into input/inbox and have the report tell us who’s winning between my favorite (Al Swearengen), my sister’s (Raylan Givens), and my little brother’s (John Dutton). All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
